title: How a TikTok-Famous Chef Feeds a Sorority | On the Job | NYT Cooking
body: The fact that kev has been doing TikTok for a few years now and still uses a “makeshift” phone stand to record is hilarious. He really is a gem 😂
author: @Eskii_NZL

title: How a TikTok-Famous Chef Feeds a Sorority | On the Job | NYT Cooking
body: Kevin knowing all the women in the sorority's names and making each plate to their tastes and requests is amazing. It shows how much respect, kindness and good food can matter in everyone's lives. A great segment!
author: @xingcat

title: How a TikTok-Famous Chef Feeds a Sorority | On the Job | NYT Cooking
body: I love how the sorority gave Kevin plenty of off days for his doctor appointments and when he just couldn’t show up to work. Shows how much they love and respect him.
author: @kam-k2269

title: How a TikTok-Famous Chef Feeds a Sorority | On the Job | NYT Cooking
body: As a portrait photographer who does sorority/fraternity composites, I was so glad to see they included him on it.  It's a high honor for a non-member to be on one.
author: @dominichanna

title: How a TikTok-Famous Chef Feeds a Sorority | On the Job | NYT Cooking
body: Single dad here... Old Cool Kevmo saved me when it comes to cooking,  his vids are short and to the point. I have cooked my kids white lasagna, regular lasagna, Italian subs, Cesar salad wraps, etc. I learned from him seasoning in layers, doing a bechamel sauce, enchiladas. I love this man!!!!
author: @smr144

title: How a TikTok-Famous Chef Feeds a Sorority | On the Job | NYT Cooking
body: I love that these girls have healthy hearty meals. Their portion sizes are great and they are developing a positive relationship with food.
author: @simplieshine

title: How a TikTok-Famous Chef Feeds a Sorority | On the Job | NYT Cooking
body: HE DESERVES THIS SO BAD. As a young cancer survivor, I love seeing him thrive. I wanted to go to culinary school, but it was too physically taxing on my body. Never gave up the love for cooking though. I screamed when I saw this in my feed ❤.
author: @celestialtay

title: How a TikTok-Famous Chef Feeds a Sorority | On the Job | NYT Cooking
body: He's their "dad away from home," making sure their bellies are fed so their brains can learn. What a wholesome piece of reporting. A breath of fresh air. Thanks! Sending Kevin so much healing energy.
author: @GT-yq7wi

title: How a TikTok-Famous Chef Feeds a Sorority | On the Job | NYT Cooking
body: "He knows your name -- It's like you're an actual  person" -- It's amazing to see how people are so starved for being seen as a whole person by others. I hope more and more people recognize the power of just being able to see each other and create real community.
author: @jaydenhartlillard

title: How a TikTok-Famous Chef Feeds a Sorority | On the Job | NYT Cooking
body: I've been following Kevin since almost the beginning and I had no idea he made special orders for each girl (I thought when he plated up stuff it was just for show). I figured it was served buffet style. That's SO MUCH work, makes me appreciate how hard he works even more.
author: @mountaineergirl255

title: How a TikTok-Famous Chef Feeds a Sorority | On the Job | NYT Cooking
body: Kevin is the Mister Rogers of the culinary world.  This couldn't have happened to a better dude!  Rootin for you Kev!
author: @iamgalaxies

title: How a TikTok-Famous Chef Feeds a Sorority | On the Job | NYT Cooking
body: Honestly, this Sorority is a model for how those of us struggling with housing need to think. I know that there are large houses that rent out rooms, with common living/kitchen areas. But including a chef and house manager to take care of issues is a great model.
author: @carabiner7999

title: How a TikTok-Famous Chef Feeds a Sorority | On the Job | NYT Cooking
body: Kevin is treating the women like restaurant guests, not institutional chow line. I did the same when I worked at an assisted living home.  After 20 years a line cook, I treated the residents like patrons not just bodies. I set my plates as I would in a restaurant. They loved it.
author: @susanrazza9740

title: How a TikTok-Famous Chef Feeds a Sorority | On the Job | NYT Cooking
body: Great video but it's also VERY FUNNY to imagine them furiously cleaning that sorority before the NYT reports arrived. No sorority or fraternity has ever been that clean and tidy, it looked like a display home
author: @funeralforahorse

title: How a TikTok-Famous Chef Feeds a Sorority | On the Job | NYT Cooking
body: His face at 6:30 when she said the tomato sauce was good made my heart melt. I love Kevin
author: @naomibourbeau2732

title: How a TikTok-Famous Chef Feeds a Sorority | On the Job | NYT Cooking
body:
Priya - Is this job your primary source of income?
Kevin - Social media is my primary source of income.
Priya - Like by a lot?
Kevin - By a lot. Aura
author: @Greeeeenleaf101

title: How a TikTok-Famous Chef Feeds a Sorority | On the Job | NYT Cooking
body: I LOVE Kevin’s videos and I about died when they said other sororities tried to poach him and he said no. GO KEVIN!!
author: @lucaswiredin

title: How a TikTok-Famous Chef Feeds a Sorority | On the Job | NYT Cooking
body: My dad also had cancer about the same time that Kevin did. Since my dad was stuck for hours doing chemo or fluid draining, plus he had rough days where he couldn't be active, we spent a lot of time sending each other short and fun video clips. Kevmo was one of my dad's favorites - not because of the shared cancer experience, but just because he was chill, making good hands-on food and had an epic beard. Sadly, my dad didn't make it, but I fondly remember him whenever I watch Kevin's videos. Food brings humans together. Live to eat, don't just eat to live.
author: @clunkclunk2099

title: How a TikTok-Famous Chef Feeds a Sorority | On the Job | NYT Cooking
body: You know when you see someone and cry because they remind you just how beautiful this world can be? Yeah, that’s Kevin.
author: @shannonzawesome6373

title: How a TikTok-Famous Chef Feeds a Sorority | On the Job | NYT Cooking
body: I was an alcoholic, severely depressed and alone and his videos got me through a lot of hard times. So happy that he’s getting his flowers. This dude rules!
author: @saturn_squared

title: The Official University of Nevada, Reno Campus Tour
body: I  STUDIED AND GRADUATED FROM UNR WAY BACK IN 1991; AND LATER EMPLOYED BY VALLEY BANK OF NEVADA (THEN), SPENT THE BEST 6 YEARS OF MY LIFE THERE.  REALLY MISSED THE PLACE, WITH SO MUCH DEVELOPMENT OVER THE YEARS
author: @rzln9780

title: The Official University of Nevada, Reno Campus Tour
body: So proud of my alma mater and how well it prepared me for life - not just for my career! #GoPack
author: @rickerwill2

title: The Official University of Nevada, Reno Campus Tour
body: I was admitted here in 2016 but because I met my girlfriend I really missed her so I left this school for the trashy San Francisco state university, was it worth it it , I don’t know but I wish I stayed at UNR way friendlier, nice campus and ethical , gf ended up leaving me , for the right reasons.

title: [Research Talk] V5 - Nguyễn Văn Chung - Đại học Nevada, Reno, Hoa Kỳ - Inspection Robots
body:
Cảm ơn anh vì nội dung của anh mạng lại, em rất thích nội dung của kênh anh.

Mong anh chia sẻ cách đọc một paper và nhận xét nó là một paper tốt hay chưa tốt. Đa phần ai theo hướng research cũng chưa tìm hiểu kĩ về phần này. Em cảm ơn anh đã xem tin nhắn.
author: @Jiro2002

title: [Research Talk] V5 - Nguyễn Văn Chung - Đại học Nevada, Reno, Hoa Kỳ - Inspection Robots
body: Xem vid mấy anh chị ngưỡng mộ quá ạ. Em học hành điểm số không đến nỗi nào nhưng thực sự kiến thức thì rất mông lung, không vững cụ thể ở đâu cả, lại thêm cái dở là cũng không thích hẳn một mảng nào đó, học cái gì mới cũng thấy hay nhưng học sâu thì lại mất tập trung. Có mỗi cái ngoại ngữ khá tốt, thấy bạn bè học cao với đi làm nhiều tiền mà tiếng yếu hơn mình lại chột dạ, không biết có phải mình đi sai đường không, cứ đâm đầu vào kỹ thuật mà chậm chạp :((
author: @lucaluca5154

title: A Day in the Life of a Resident
body: If you are interested in medicine or are already in medicine, join our official medical discord here https://discord.gg/CYCvEdN . Called the Medical Knowledge Association, where you learn and ask questions for free! The Live version of the student doctor network.

title: A Day in the Life of a Clinical Psychology PhD Student
body: i love tattoos and piercings and am into academia as well, glad to see some representation for us :)) keep going you got this!
author: @nonbinaryentity

title: A Day in the Life of a Clinical Psychology PhD Student
body: thank you for this video nursing majors are the only ones who say their major is tiring than everyone elses and that theyre better than everyone elses clinical is one of the hardest degrees out there ❤️ it can be mentally draining but its worth it and at least we dont have the highest drop out rate .
author: @chlochlo8207

title: A Day in the Life of a Clinical Psychology PhD Student
body: Would love to connect or possibly have an informational interview. You seem awesome and very similar to what I want to do
author: @EthanIrwin-34

title: A Day in the Life of a Clinical Psychology PhD Student
body: wow honestly im in my first year of college (finishing in less than a month horray!!) and im thinking i definitely want to do something in psychology. Ive looked at psychologist, clinical psychologist, criminologist, criminal psychologist, and so on and so forth. the only thing thats holding me back from pursuing some on those is that most of them need a phd and i don’t know- im not dumb or anything but i have average grades. im a: an A, couple of Bs, maybe a C, type of student, but its always scared me. Ive always known how to be my own advocate and i always study for my things but like i always thought im not exactly smart enough to become a doctor. like earning a phd is something only smart people do. Im trying to tell myself that im wrong and that if i think its what i want to do then i should just do it and try my best.. its better to stress while doing the work then to stress on the regret of not doing it at all right? anyways just rantingg.
author: @sunoosblush

title: A Day in the Life of a Clinical Psychology PhD Student
body: Good luck!  Interesting video!   Many doctoral students and graduates are struggling with huge student loan debts and very stressful early career experiences.   It can very disappointing...
